workflows.title: Verified Erdős 64 prefix

stages.title: Official Erdős 64 statement
stages.summary: The pinned public boundary asks for a power-of-two cycle in every finite graph of minimum degree at least three.

primaryDeclaration.type: Prop
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.OfficialStatement

stages.title: Executable internal problem
stages.summary: The same Mathlib graph is paired with an explicit finite inspection order and an executable but equivalent length predicate.

primaryDeclaration.type: (V : Type u) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun _object => Unit
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.staticInput

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ (length : ℕ), Internal.PowerOfTwoLength length ↔ ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.powerOfTwoLength_iff

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Target object ↔ ∃ exponent vertex cycle, exponent ≥ 2 ∧ cycle.IsCycle ∧ cycle.length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.target_iff_official_conclusion

stages.title: K₄ Mersenne-return fixture
stages.summary: The explicit four-cycle is converted to a length-three return in the graph with its root edge deleted.

primaryDeclaration.type: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength Tests.k4.graph Internal.PowerOfTwoLength
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4Cycle

evidenceDeclarations.type: Internal.Baseline Tests.k4
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4_baseline

evidenceDeclarations.type: Tests.fourCycleWalk.IsCycle
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.fourCycleWalk_isCycle

evidenceDeclarations.type: Internal.PowerOfTwoLength Tests.fourCycleWalk.length
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.fourCycleWalk_powerOfTwo

evidenceDeclarations.type: Internal.MersenneReturn Tests.k4.graph
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4MersenneReturn

stages.title: CT1 Mersenne target algebra
stages.summary: CT1 validates one rooted return or constructs the exact avoiding residual from disjoint return sets.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : Internal.Object V) →
    (baseline : Internal.Baseline object) →
      ¬Internal.Target object →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedAvoidingRun (Internal.ct1Spec V) (Internal.ct1Input object baseline)
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runAvoidingCT1

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V), Internal.Target object ↔ Internal.HasMersenneReturn object.graph
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.target_iff_hasMersenneReturn

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  ¬Internal.Target object ↔
    ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), Disjoint (Internal.returnSet object.graph dart) Internal.MersenneSet
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.not_target_iff_returnSets_disjoint

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : Internal.Object V) →
    (baseline : Internal.Baseline object) →
      Internal.MersenneReturn object.graph →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedC1Run (Internal.ct1Spec V) (Internal.ct1Input object baseline)
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runMersenneCT1

evidenceDeclarations.type: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedC1Run (Internal.ct1Spec (Fin 4)) (Internal.ct1Input Tests.k4 Tests.k4_baseline)
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4MersenneCT1Run

stages.title: CT2 no proper core
stages.summary: A supplied proper finite subgraph is one certified local reduction; target transport and lexicographic minimality force its minimum degree to be at most two.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedNoProperCorePrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedNoProperCorePrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G), subgraph.value.object.minDegree ≤ 2
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properSubgraph_minDegree_le_two

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (Internal.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).terminal =
    StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properCoreCT2Run_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (Internal.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).trace =
    [StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properCoreCT2Run_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (minimumDegreeThree : 3 ≤ subgraph.value.object.minDegree),
  (Internal.properCoreCT2Run ctx subgraph minimumDegreeThree).checks = 1
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.properCoreCT2Run_checks

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀
  (ctx :
    StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.packedStaticInput.problem
      Internal.packedStaticInput.Target),
  Internal.VerifiedNoProperCorePrefix ctx → Internal.VerifiedCT1CT2Prefix (Internal.packedStaticInput.fixedContext ctx)
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.noProperCorePrefix_previous

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  ∃ run,
    run.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2 ∧
      run.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run_total

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  (input.properSubgraphCT2Run ctx subgraph baseline).checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).size
            (input.properSubgraphCT2Input ctx subgraph baseline) +
          1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).degree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run_polynomial

stages.title: Routed CT2 deletion criticality
stages.summary: The certified CT1 residual enters local CT2 discovery; its exact disabled result forces every edge to have a degree-three endpoint.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx, (Internal.problem V).rank ctx.G ≤ (Internal.problem V).rank object ∧ Internal.VerifiedCT1CT2Prefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedCT1CT2Prefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target),
  ∃ (reject : ∀ (a : (Internal.localRoute ctx).Seed (Internal.ct1AvoidingSource ctx)), False),
    (Internal.localRoute ctx).discover (Internal.ct1AvoidingSource ctx) =
      StructuralExhaustion.Core.Routing.Discovery.disabled reject
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.localRoute_disabled

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target)
  (dart : ctx.G.graph.Dart),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.2 = 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.deletionCriticality

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target)
  {left right : V},
  4 ≤ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G left →
    4 ≤ StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G right → ¬ctx.G.graph.Adj left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.highDegree_independent

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext (Internal.problem V) Internal.Target),
  Internal.VerifiedCT1CT2Prefix ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.verifiedCT1CT2Prefix

stages.title: CT3 boundaried replacement
stages.summary: Literal packed-graph CT3 gluing derives whole-rank decrease, minimum-degree preservation, and target transport from local replacement data.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.packedStaticInput

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        left ≠
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        right →
    ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
        left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.boundaryDegreeProfile_ne_not_targetComplete

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries left
      right →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ContextEquivalent input boundaries
      left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.targetComplete_contextUniversal
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_terminal
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} {T : Type u} {boundaries : FinEnum T}
  [inst : Nonempty T] {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target}
  {atom :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom input boundaries ctx}
  (compression :
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression input boundaries atom),
  ∃ result,
    result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.Terminal.compression ∧
      result.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.vectorComputation,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionSearch,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Graph.NodeId.compressionTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_total
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_checks
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.run_polynomial

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) {replacement source : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T}
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  replacement.LexSmaller source →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries replacement outside).lexRank <
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries source outside).lexRank
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_lexRank_lt

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) [Nonempty T] (threshold : ℕ)
  (source replacement : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  (∀ (boundary : T),
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries replacement boundary =
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries source boundary) →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries threshold replacement →
      threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph source outside).minDegree →
        threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph replacement outside).minDegree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_preserves_minDegree
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.impossible

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.VerifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix ctx → Internal.VerifiedNoProperCorePrefix ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.boundariedReplacementPrefix_previous

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.VerifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix ctx →
    ∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) [inst : Nonempty T] (atom : Internal.ConcreteCT3.ProperAtom ctx boundaries)
      (compression : Internal.ConcreteCT3.Compression atom), False
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.boundariedReplacementPrefix_uncompressible

stages.title: CT1 induced-P₁₃ branch
stages.summary: One CT1 block covers diagram nodes [15]–[16]: literal P₁₃-freeness reaches the avoiding terminal, HSS closes it against dyadic-target avoidance, and the forced embedding reaches C1.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedInducedP13Prefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedInducedP13Prefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) [inst : Fintype V] [inst_1 : DecidableRel G.Adj],
  3 ≤ G.minDegree →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathFree G 13 →
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HasCycleWithLength G fun length => ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.External.HegdeSandeepShashank.p13Free_hasPowerOfTwoCycle

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (free : Internal.P13Free ctx),
  (Internal.runP13FreeCT1 ctx free).result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.avoiding
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13FreeCT1_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (free : Internal.P13Free ctx),
  (Internal.runP13FreeCT1 ctx free).result.trace =
    [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.avoidingTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13FreeCT1_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.P13Free ctx →
    ∃ run,
      run.result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.avoiding ∧
        run.result.trace =
          [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
            StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision,
            StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.avoidingTerminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13FreeCT1_total

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (free : Internal.P13Free ctx),
  (Internal.runP13FreeCT1 ctx free).checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedAvoidingBudget Internal.inducedP13Profile.encoding.spec).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedAvoidingBudget Internal.inducedP13Profile.encoding.spec).size
            (Internal.inducedP13Input ctx) +
          1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedAvoidingBudget Internal.inducedP13Profile.encoding.spec).degree
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13FreeCT1_polynomial

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.P13Free ctx → Internal.PackedTarget ctx.G
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.hssTarget_of_p13Free

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.P13Free ctx → False
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.p13FreeBranch_closed

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.HasInducedP13 ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.inducedP13_of_hss

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (certificate : Internal.InducedP13Certificate ctx),
  (Internal.runInducedP13CT1 ctx certificate).result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.c1
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runInducedP13CT1_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (certificate : Internal.InducedP13Certificate ctx),
  (Internal.runInducedP13CT1 ctx certificate).result.trace =
    [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
      StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.c1Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runInducedP13CT1_trace

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (certificate : Internal.InducedP13Certificate ctx),
  ∃ run,
    run.result.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.Terminal.c1 ∧
      run.result.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.equivalenceCertification,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.realizationDecision, StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Graph.NodeId.c1Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runInducedP13CT1_total

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (certificate : Internal.InducedP13Certificate ctx),
  (Internal.runInducedP13CT1 ctx certificate).checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedC1Budget Internal.inducedP13Profile.encoding.spec).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedC1Budget Internal.inducedP13Profile.encoding.spec).size
            (Internal.inducedP13Input ctx) +
          1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT1.certifiedC1Budget Internal.inducedP13Profile.encoding.spec).degree

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.VerifiedInducedP13Prefix ctx → Internal.VerifiedBoundariedReplacementPrefix ctx

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  Internal.VerifiedInducedP13Prefix ctx → Internal.HasInducedP13 ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.inducedP13Prefix_realization

stages.title: CT12 induced-P₁₃ packing
stages.summary: One CT12 stage selects a maximum vertex-disjoint induced-P₁₃ family, derives maximal saturation, audits exactly that selected list, and constructs the P₁₃-free remainder containing no finite subgraph of minimum degree three.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedP13PackingPrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedP13PackingPrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  (Internal.runP13PackingCT12 ctx).terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT12.Graph.Terminal.exhausted
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13PackingCT12_terminal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  (Internal.runP13PackingCT12 ctx).iterations ≤ FinEnum.card ctx.G.Vertex
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13PackingCT12_iterations

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  (Internal.runP13PackingCT12 ctx).iterations = Internal.p13 ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13PackingCT12_iterations_exact

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExecutionResult.trace
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ListPeeling.capability Internal.PackedProblem
          (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.Window ctx.G.object 13))
        (Internal.runP13PackingCT12 ctx)).length ≤
    4 * FinEnum.card ctx.G.Vertex + 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runP13PackingCT12_trace_bound

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (other : Internal.InducedP13Packing ctx), (↑other).card ≤ Internal.p13 ctx
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.p13_maximum

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget)
  (window : Internal.InducedP13Window ctx),
  ∃ selected ∈ Internal.p13Windows ctx,
    ¬Disjoint (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.support ctx.G.object 13 window)
        (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathPacking.support ctx.G.object 13 selected)
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.p13_saturated

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget),
  (Internal.p13RemainderVertices ctx).card + 13 * Internal.p13 ctx = FinEnum.card ctx.G.Vertex
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.p13Remainder_partition

    ∃ position, path position = vertex ∧ left ≤ position ∧ position ≤ right

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  {G : SimpleGraph V} →
    {order : ℕ} →
      (LengthOK : ℕ → Prop) →
        (path : SimpleGraph.pathGraph order ↪g G) →
          (leftOutside connector rightOutside : V) →
            (∀ (position : Fin order), leftOutside ≠ path position) →
              (∀ (position : Fin order), connector ≠ path position) →
                (∀ (position : Fin order), rightOutside ≠ path position) →
                  leftOutside ≠ rightOutside →
                    G.Adj leftOutside connector →
                      G.Adj connector rightOutside →
                        (left right : Fin order) →
                          left ≤ right →
                            G.Adj rightOutside (path left) →
                              G.Adj leftOutside (path right) →
                                LengthOK (4 + (↑right - ↑left)) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength G LengthOK
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathBridge.connectorCycle

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  {G : SimpleGraph V} →
    {order : ℕ} →
      (LengthOK : ℕ → Prop) →
        (path : SimpleGraph.pathGraph order ↪g G) →
          (leftOutside rightOutside : V) →
            (∀ (position : Fin order), leftOutside ≠ path position) →
              (∀ (position : Fin order), rightOutside ≠ path position) →
                leftOutside ≠ rightOutside →
                  (first second third fourth : Fin order) →
                    first < second →
                      second < third →
                        third < fourth →
                          G.Adj leftOutside (path first) →
                            G.Adj rightOutside (path second) →
                              G.Adj leftOutside (path third) →
                                G.Adj rightOutside (path fourth) →
                                  LengthOK (4 + (↑second - ↑first) + (↑fourth - ↑third)) →
                                    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength G LengthOK

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (pair : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanWindowCycle.ClosedPair path),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanWindowCycle.DirectCycleFree LengthOK pair ↔
    ¬LengthOK (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.pairCycleLength pair.left.first pair.left.second) ∧
      ¬LengthOK (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.pairCycleLength pair.right.first pair.right.second) ∧
        (∀ (leftSide rightSide : Bool),
            ¬LengthOK
                (4 +
                  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.positionDistance (pair.left.endpoint leftSide)
                    (pair.right.endpoint rightSide))) ∧
          (pair.left.first < pair.right.first →
              pair.right.first < pair.left.second →
                pair.left.second < pair.right.second →
                  ¬LengthOK (4 + (↑pair.right.first - ↑pair.left.first) + (↑pair.right.second - ↑pair.left.second))) ∧
            (pair.right.first < pair.left.first →
              pair.left.first < pair.right.second →
                pair.right.second < pair.left.second →
                  ¬LengthOK (4 + (↑pair.left.first - ↑pair.right.first) + (↑pair.left.second - ↑pair.right.second)))

stages.title: Exact assigned charge, receiver discharge, and boundary split
stages.summary: Lean transfers every candidate weight and half-credit to literal induced-core charge and proves the exact post-selection decomposition. Independently of local resolution or B2 disjointness, deleting all actual high centers leaves a literal P13-free, core-free, subcubic graph. Its exact receiver overload gives the unconditional bound -TypeBNet ≤ 21 assigned-surplus + TypeA-overload.

  (∀ (demand : Demand) (candidate : profile.Candidate demand),
      refinedSupport demand candidate ⊆ profile.carrierSupport demand candidate) →

        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.AssignedSupportCharge.Profile.centerQuarterChargeAt ctx.G.object ↑demand +
      ∑ vertex ∈ support.usedCore choice, support.assignedChargeProfile.coreQuarterChargeAt vertex
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport.fullLocalQuarterBalance_le_actualCharge

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}
  (support : Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport ctx)
  (choice : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteRefinedLedger.Profile.FullChoice support.completionProfile),
  0 ≤
    support.assignedChargeProfile.centerQuarterCharge +
      ∑ vertex ∈ support.usedCore choice, support.assignedChargeProfile.coreQuarterChargeAt vertex
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport.processedFanCharge_nonnegative

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}
  (support : Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport ctx)
  (choice : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteRefinedLedger.Profile.FullChoice support.completionProfile),
  0 ≤ support.assignedChargeProfile.netQuarterCharge ∨ support.remainingQuarterCharge choice < 0
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport.netQuarterCharge_nonnegative_or_remaining_negative

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (support : Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport ctx)
  (choice : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteRefinedLedger.Profile.FullChoice support.completionProfile),
  support.remainingInducedQuarterCharge choice =
    support.remainingQuarterCharge choice + support.remainingBoundaryCredit choice
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport.remainingInducedQuarterCharge_eq_raw_add_boundary

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (support : Internal.TypeBAssignedSupport ctx)
  (choice : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteRefinedLedger.Profile.FullChoice support.completionProfile),
  support.RemainingUnsaturated choice →
    ∀ (transfer : support.BoundaryTransfer choice), 0 ≤ support.assignedChargeProfile.netQuarterCharge

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (candidate : (Internal.TypeBLocalEntry.selectionProfile reserve entry).Candidate),
  (Internal.TypeBLocalEntry.actualCoreSupport reserve entry candidate).card ≤ 24

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext Internal.PackedProblem Internal.PackedTarget}

  ∑ vertex ∈ support.processedCore choice, ctx.G.object.degree vertex ≤ 200 * support.centers.card

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  support.RemainingUnsaturated choice →
    -support.assignedChargeProfile.netQuarterCharge ≤ 800 * ↑support.assignedChargeProfile.assignedSurplus

    21 * ↑scope.highCenterChargeProfile.assignedSurplus + ↑scope.centerDeletedReceiverOverload

      Nonempty support.MinimalOverlap ∨
        ∃ choice,
          0 ≤ support.assignedChargeProfile.netQuarterCharge ∨
            Nonempty (support.RemainingSaturated choice) ∨
              support.assignedChargeProfile.netQuarterCharge < 0 ∧

stages.title: CT12 two-degenerate sparse envelope
stages.summary: Deletion criticality supplies a cubic vertex. The no-proper-core theorem makes its literal complement 2-degenerate; CT12 audits the proof-selected elimination list exactly once per remaining vertex, yielding m ≤ 2n-2 and the exact sparse slack/surplus identities.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedSparseEnvelopePrefix ctx
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.exists_verifiedSparseEnvelopePrefix

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (bound : ℕ),
  object.InternalMinDegreeFree (bound + 1) →
    Nonempty (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.DegeneracyPeeling.Certificate object bound)
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.DegeneracyPeeling.exists_certificate_of_internalMinDegreeFree

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V),
  object.InternalMinDegreeFree 3 → 2 ≤ FinEnum.card V → object.edgeCount ≤ 2 * FinEnum.card V - 3
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.DegeneracyPeeling.edgeCount_le_two_mul_vertexCount_sub_three

evidenceDeclarations.type:

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V) (bound : ℕ),
  (∀ (vertex : V), bound ≤ object.degree vertex) →
    ↑(List.map (fun vertex => object.degree vertex - bound) object.input.vertices.orderedValues).sum =
      2 * ↑object.edgeCount - ↑bound * ↑(FinEnum.card V)
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.SurplusPortActivity.degreeExcess_sum_int_eq

stages.title: CT15 baseline-spine demand
stages.summary: The exact cubic-baseline skeleton count and integer bit budget are computed from n. The canonical empty coordinate family realizes the baseline-demand definition with its full exact deficit, and CT15 certifies its full-rank ledger in linear work. No linear deficit estimate is assumed.

primaryDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Baseline object →
    ¬Internal.Target object →
      ∃ ctx,
        Internal.PackedProblem.rank ctx.G ≤
            Internal.PackedProblem.rank (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.pack object) ∧
          Internal.VerifiedBaselineSpineDemandPrefix ctx

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (∀ (coordinate : S.Coordinate), ¬S.TargetDependent input coordinate) →
    StructuralExhaustion.CT15.ledgerTotal S capability input ≤ S.capacity input →
      (StructuralExhaustion.CT15.run S capability input).terminal =
        StructuralExhaustion.CT15.Graph.Terminal.fullRankLedger

workflows.purpose: Inspect the exact Mersenne target algebra, lexicographic minimal selection, CT2 criticality and bridge contraction, boundaried replacement, the HSS-forced induced-P13 CT1 stage, the routed maximum-packing CT12 remainder stage, the exhaustive CT10 P13 attachment-label algebra, the ordered CT6 degree-surplus ledger, the routed CT9 surplus-pair split, and the Type B development through its graph-derived high-center choice/overlap trichotomy.

links.label: literal B1 incidences generate the candidate fibre

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {base : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun x => Unit}
  {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V} {baseline : base.problem.Baseline object} {center : V}
  (fanProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.FanWindowProfile V)
  (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.IncidenceReserve V),
  (∑ incidence ∈ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.profile fanProfile reserve).allItems,
      if
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.incidenceKind fanProfile incidence =
            StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.IncidenceKind.nonWindow then
        2
      else 0) =
    ↑(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.nonWindowQuarterCredit fanProfile)

evidenceDeclarations.type:

  (∀ (demand : Demand) (candidate : profile.Candidate demand),
      refinedSupport demand candidate ⊆ profile.carrierSupport demand candidate) →
    ∀ (left right : { demand // demand ∈ selected }),
      ↑left ≠ ↑right →
        Disjoint (refinedSupport (↑left) (choice.entry left)) (refinedSupport (↑right) (choice.entry right))

example.title: Erdős Problem 64
example.summary: A partial proof with exact Mersenne returns, lexicographic minimal selection, CT2 criticality and bridge contraction, CT3 boundaried uncompressibility, an HSS-forced induced-P13 CT1 stage, a routed maximum-packing CT12 remainder stage, the complete CT10 P13 attachment-label algebra, a polynomial ordered CT6 degree-surplus ledger, a routed CT9 surplus-pair split, framework-native local-port verification, concrete Type B candidate fibres, an unconditional dependent CT12 choice-or-minimal-overlap theorem, an exact high-center local-resolution trichotomy, a no-double-counted induced-core realization of the full Type B charge ledger, and the choice-free Type B receiver-overload deficit bound.

proofSteps.workBound: Statement boundary only; no computation is performed.
proofSteps.title: Official theorem boundary
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The public statement is exact, but the current verified workflow is explicitly a partial prefix and does not yet prove this proposition.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The public Lean proposition uses the same finite simple graph and asks for exactly the power-of-two cycle asserted by the manuscript's main theorem.

declarationGroups.title: Public mathematical statement
declarationGroups.explanation: This declaration is the exact Mathlib-facing proposition that the completed formalization must prove.

declarations.type: Prop
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.OfficialStatement

proofSteps.workBound: The exponent search is bounded by the candidate cycle length.
proofSteps.title: Faithful executable target
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Finite enumeration and adjacency decisions control execution order only; they do not introduce a second graph representation or weaken the theorem.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Lean equips the same Mathlib graph with finite inspection data and replaces the unbounded exponent quantifier by a bounded decidable search, then proves that this executable target is equivalent to the official conclusion.

declarationGroups.title: Encoding and equivalence bridge
declarationGroups.explanation: The static input supplies deterministic finite data; the two equivalence theorems prove that neither the cycle predicate nor its exponent range has changed.

declarations.type: (V : Type u) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun _object => Unit
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.staticInput

declarations.type: ∀ (length : ℕ), Internal.PowerOfTwoLength length ↔ ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.powerOfTwoLength_iff

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  Internal.Target object ↔ ∃ exponent vertex cycle, exponent ≥ 2 ∧ cycle.IsCycle ∧ cycle.length = 2 ^ exponent
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.target_iff_official_conclusion

proofSteps.workBound: One fixed four-vertex certificate.
proofSteps.title: Concrete K₄ execution fixture
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is implementation support rather than a manuscript proof step; no general claim is inferred from K₄.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A four-cycle in K₄ is converted into a length-three return after deleting its root edge, providing a closed smoke test for the target encoding and CT1 execution.

declarationGroups.title: Concrete certificate and checks
declarationGroups.explanation: These declarations construct K₄, prove its baseline and four-cycle facts, and pin the corresponding Mersenne-return certificate. They test the general implementation but are not used as proof of the general theorem.

declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4Cycle

declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.fourCycleWalk_isCycle

declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.fourCycleWalk_powerOfTwo

declarations.type: Internal.MersenneReturn Tests.k4.graph
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Tests.k4MersenneReturn

proofSteps.workBound: One certificate check for C1 and zero checks for the proof-carrying avoiding execution.
proofSteps.title: Mersenne return target algebra
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This step proves the manuscript's target dictionary and executes both CT1 branches; it does not yet rule out the target-avoiding branch.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Removing one edge from a power-of-two cycle leaves a return path whose length is one less than a power of two. Conversely, adjoining the root edge to such a return produces the target cycle. CT1 records either a supplied return or universal avoidance.
proofSteps.formalStatement: G \text{ has a } C_{2^j} \iff \exists e,\ 2^j-1 \in R_e(G)

declarationGroups.title: Exact graph-theoretic equivalences
declarationGroups.explanation: These theorems identify the internal target with existence of a Mersenne return and identify target avoidance with disjointness of every return set from the Mersenne lengths.

declarations.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V), Internal.Target object ↔ Internal.HasMersenneReturn object.graph
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.target_iff_hasMersenneReturn

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  ¬Internal.Target object ↔
    ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), Disjoint (Internal.returnSet object.graph dart) Internal.MersenneSet
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.not_target_iff_returnSets_disjoint

declarationGroups.title: Positive and avoiding CT1 executions
declarationGroups.explanation: The positive run validates one proof-carrying return; the avoiding run retains the exact universal non-realization residual. The K₄ run pins the positive path on a concrete input.

proofSteps.workBound: Exactly one local certificate check; degree-zero polynomial budget.
proofSteps.title: No proper minimum-degree-three core
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The framework checks one proof-specified proper subgraph at a time; the theorem universally quantifies over certificates and does not enumerate subgraphs.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: If a proper subgraph still had minimum degree at least three, target avoidance would be inherited and it would be a lexicographically smaller counterexample. Certified CT2 reduction formalizes this contradiction for one arbitrary supplied proper subgraph.

declarationGroups.title: Mathematical conclusion
declarationGroups.explanation: This theorem is the direct Lean form of the manuscript lemma: every proper finite subgraph of the selected counterexample has minimum degree at most two.

      StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal]

proofSteps.workBound: At most one scan of the explicit dart list; a closing deletion certificate uses one check.
proofSteps.title: Edge-deletion criticality
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This matches the manuscript deletion argument. The registered route is implementation provenance, not an additional mathematical hypothesis.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: An edge with both endpoints of degree at least four could be deleted while preserving minimum degree three, and deletion cannot create a new cycle. Minimality therefore forces every edge to touch a degree-three vertex.
proofSteps.formalStatement: xy \in E(G) \Longrightarrow d_G(x)=3 \lor d_G(y)=3;\qquad V_{\ge 4}(G) \text{ is independent}

declarationGroups.title: Degree-three endpoint consequences
declarationGroups.explanation: The first theorem proves the degree-three endpoint alternative for every edge; the second derives independence of the vertices of degree at least four.

declarations.type:

  (dart : ctx.G.graph.Dart),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject.degree ctx.G dart.toProd.2 = 3
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.deletionCriticality

declarations.type:

proofSteps.workBound: Proof-level literal gluing and one certified reduction; no universe of contexts, graphs, or replacements is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Boundaried replacement and uncompressibility
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This covers nodes [11]--[14] for normalized decompositions with nonempty boundary. Empty-boundary closed representatives remain a separate manuscript branch. Later invocations at [36]--[39] are not yet formalized.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A proper boundaried atom cannot be replaced by a strictly smaller representative with the same boundary degrees and no worse response in every compatible outside context. Such a replacement would preserve the baseline and target avoidance and contradict minimality.

declarationGroups.title: Boundary and replacement theorems
declarationGroups.explanation: These declarations separate unequal boundary-degree fibres, prove universal context response, derive global graph obligations from literal gluing, and conclude hereditary target-uncompressibility.

declarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        left ≠
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        right →
    ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
        left right
declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.boundaryDegreeProfile_ne_not_targetComplete

declarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries left
      right →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ContextEquivalent input boundaries
      left right
declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.targetComplete_contextUniversal

declarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) {replacement source : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T}
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  replacement.LexSmaller source →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries replacement outside).lexRank <

proofSteps.title: Forced induced P₁₃
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The corollary is fully formalized relative to the explicitly isolated HSS theorem. The app must display that trust boundary separately from kernel-proved consequences.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: CT1 tests for an induced P₁₃. Its avoiding residual is literal P₁₃-freeness, which the Hegde--Sandeep--Shashank theorem turns into a forbidden power-of-two cycle. Therefore the minimal counterexample contains an induced P₁₃, recorded by a proof-carrying graph embedding.

declarationGroups.title: Trusted external closure
declarationGroups.explanation: This isolated axiom is exactly the cited Hegde--Sandeep--Shashank theorem: finite P₁₃-free minimum-degree-three graphs contain a power-of-two cycle. It is the sole trusted external theorem in the repository.

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) [inst : Fintype V] [inst_1 : DecidableRel G.Adj],
  3 ≤ G.minDegree →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathFree G 13 →
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HasCycleWithLength G fun length => ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.External.HegdeSandeepShashank.p13Free_hasPowerOfTwoCycle

proofSteps.plainExplanation: An outside vertex is labelled by the path positions to which it is adjacent. Target avoidance forbids pairs of positions at gaps two and six. CT10 exhaustively classifies all 8192 bit codes, obtaining exactly 399 legal labels and the manuscript relations Cₛ and Ω₂.

proofSteps.title: Bridgelessness by exact contraction
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This proves that every dart is non-bridging. The following framework transition consumes that result to construct the triangular-port return.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: For a hypothetical bridge uv, the framework removes v, reattaches its non-bridge incidences to u, proves that no degree is lost, and lifts every contracted simple cycle with the same length. The smaller target-avoiding graph contradicts packed minimality through CT2.
proofSteps.formalStatement: e\in E(G)\Longrightarrow e\text{ is not a bridge}

proofSteps.workBound: At most 6n²+3 CT10 classification and row checks on the explicit shoulder-site × declared-vertex table; no path, subgraph, graph, or context universe is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: First landing exhaustion for triangular shoulders
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This proves node [80] for every actual completion incidence and for the verified return's first noncentral completion. Cross-shoulder multiplicity at node [81] is the next stage.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Every actual shoulder-completion incidence is classified by CT10. Four-cycle freeness rules out every noncentral neighbour of the fan centre; completion-edge exclusions rule out its own port data; the remaining fan-core endpoint is exactly a shoulder of another triangular port, and every other endpoint is outside. The framework also applies this classification to the first noncentral completion on the verified return path.

proofSteps.workBound: At most five primitive CT9 partition checks on four explicit shoulder pairs and one unit label; no pair set, path, subgraph, or graph universe is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Cross-shoulder multiplicity
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This proves node [81] with the high-shoulder branch kept explicit. It does not assume every shoulder is cubic; that hypothesis merely forces the supplied bounded CT9 constructor.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: For two distinct triangular ports, CT9 scans their four possible shoulder pairs as one capacity-one fibre. Distinct port endpoints are nonadjacent and the shoulder pairs are disjoint. Two cross edges sharing a shoulder force four distinct neighbours there; two disjoint cross edges form a four-cycle. Since four-cycles are excluded, either a high shoulder is routed to the surplus branch or at most one cross edge survives.
proofSteps.formalStatement: |E(S_i,S_j)|\ge2\Longrightarrow (\exists s\in S_i\cup S_j,\ d_G(s)\ge4)\ \lor\ C_4\subseteq G

proofSteps.title: Fan-compatible pairs give fan-closed local data
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This proves the local fan-closure block at node [72]. The logically later claim that a failed global disjoint choice yields a minimal Type-B overlap obstruction remains with lem:typeB-bridge-to-overlap, after its prerequisites.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The P13 packing defines the window side and its literal complement. For two distinct fan-compatible open ports whose four oriented non-centre incidences are assigned to the Type-B envelope, CT5 checks those four sites. Both endpoint remainder facts and all assignment facts are consumed to derive two fan-closed ports; endpoint injectivity and the two-shoulder normal form prove that the four carriers are distinct.

proofSteps.title: Compatible-pair cubic-closed mass and deficit
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This verifies the actual cubic-closed count and positive-deficit arithmetic in the local routing proposition. The hybrid B1 half-credit capacity and the later global B2 disjointness/overlap alternatives are not claimed by this stage.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The prior CT5 charge residual is routed to CT14 without changing the selected graph or branch state. CT14's members are precisely the actual vertices adjacent to the centre that are cubic, lie in the P13-packing remainder, and have every non-centre incidence assigned. Each fan-closed port endpoint satisfies this predicate. Endpoint injectivity embeds Bool into the member subtype, so its cardinality and Unit-label multiplicity are at least two; for centre degree k at least four, the quarter-deficit numerator 4c+k-11 is at least k-3 and is positive.

proofSteps.title: Hybrid window/non-window incidence budget
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This verifies the local incidence universe, carrier disjointness, exact hybrid multiplicities, and half-credit arithmetic. It deliberately takes k≤8 as the marked-fan branch input. The later identification of this local entry with the manuscript's global B1 alternative and the B2 overlap/blocker closure are not claimed.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The actual cubic-closed subtype from the preceding CT14 result is refined to exactly two non-centre incidences per member. Four-cycle avoidance makes their non-centre endpoints pairwise distinct. A second CT14 ledger labels every incidence as window or non-window, records exact multiplicities, and gives every incidence two quarter-units. Under the marked-fan bound k≤8, the total 4c quarter-units exceed the deficit 4c+k-11 by at least three; after applying all window credit, the non-window credit pays the remaining demand.

The graph layer constructs the literal two-per-member incidence universe, proves graph-theoretic endpoint disjointness, runs CT14, proves exact binary-label multiplicities and deficit arithmetic, and records totality and polynomial work.

proofSteps.workBound: The CT1 avoiding execution performs zero realization checks. A positive branch validates one already constructed cycle in one check. All path segments are symbolic Mathlib walks; no labels, walks, paths, vertex tuples, subsets, subgraphs, or graphs are enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Direct fan-window cycle eliminations
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is the complete same-window direct-cycle lemma. The formal statement makes the necessary condition h∉V(P) explicit; the manuscript now records that ordinary remainder-side fan centers satisfy it and that packed-window handoff centers use the grouped-envelope branch. The distinct two-window lemma and global B1/B2 bridge remain downstream.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: For two distinct external neighbours closed on the same induced path window, the framework expands the exact direct-cycle-free clauses. An internal forbidden gap closes through one external vertex; a forbidden cross-distance closes through the two-edge fan path; interlacing endpoints close through two disjoint induced-path segments. Each branch is a literal Mathlib simple cycle. On the target-avoiding selected graph, the certificate-driven CT1 avoiding run proves that no such violation exists.

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned direct-cycle CT1 profile
declarationGroups.explanation: The graph layer owns ordered segment support bounds, one-segment connector cycles, two-segment interlacing cycles, the exact finite violation type, its arithmetic equivalence, cycle construction, CT1 execution, trace, totality, and the zero-check avoiding result. The Erdős layer supplies only the power-of-two predicate and selected target-avoiding context.

          (leftOutside connector rightOutside : V) →
            (∀ (position : Fin order), leftOutside ≠ path position) →
              (∀ (position : Fin order), connector ≠ path position) →
                (∀ (position : Fin order), rightOutside ≠ path position) →
                  leftOutside ≠ rightOutside →
                    G.Adj leftOutside connector →
                      G.Adj connector rightOutside →
                        (left right : Fin order) →
                          left ≤ right →

                                LengthOK (4 + (↑right - ↑left)) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength G LengthOK

declarations.type:
{V : Type u} →

          (leftOutside rightOutside : V) →
            (∀ (position : Fin order), leftOutside ≠ path position) →
              (∀ (position : Fin order), rightOutside ≠ path position) →
                leftOutside ≠ rightOutside →
                  (first second third fourth : Fin order) →
                    first < second →
                      second < third →
                        third < fourth →
                          G.Adj leftOutside (path first) →
                            G.Adj rightOutside (path second) →
                              G.Adj leftOutside (path third) →
                                G.Adj rightOutside (path fourth) →
                                  LengthOK (4 + (↑second - ↑first) + (↑fourth - ↑third)) →
                                    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CycleWithLength G LengthOK

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {G : SimpleGraph V} {order : ℕ} {path : SimpleGraph.pathGraph order ↪g G} (LengthOK : ℕ → Prop)
  (pair : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanWindowCycle.ClosedPair path),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanWindowCycle.DirectCycleFree LengthOK pair ↔
    ¬LengthOK (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.pairCycleLength pair.left.first pair.left.second) ∧
      ¬LengthOK (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.pairCycleLength pair.right.first pair.right.second) ∧
        (∀ (leftSide rightSide : Bool),
            ¬LengthOK
                (4 +
                  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathAttachment.positionDistance (pair.left.endpoint leftSide)
                    (pair.right.endpoint rightSide))) ∧
          (pair.left.first < pair.right.first →
              pair.right.first < pair.left.second →
                pair.left.second < pair.right.second →
                  ¬LengthOK (4 + (↑pair.right.first - ↑pair.left.first) + (↑pair.right.second - ↑pair.left.second))) ∧
            (pair.right.first < pair.left.first →
              pair.left.first < pair.right.second →
                pair.right.second < pair.left.second →
                  ¬LengthOK (4 + (↑pair.left.first - ↑pair.right.first) + (↑pair.left.second - ↑pair.right.second)))

proofSteps.title: Two-window direct cycle eliminations
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is the complete distinct-two-window direct-cycle lemma. The positive-deficit arithmetic and global B1/B2 carrier bridge remain downstream.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: For two external vertices incident with two vertex-disjoint induced windows, the graph layer constructs an orientation-independent bridge through each window. The bridge tails are disjoint because window supports are disjoint and both external vertices lie outside them. Their concatenation is therefore a literal simple cycle of exact length 4+|i-j|+|a-b|. The selected target-avoiding context excludes every accepted total through a zero-check CT1 run.

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned two-window CT1 profile
declarationGroups.explanation: The graph layer owns the orientation-independent bridge, its exact length and tail-support classification, the two-window data contract, the literal simple-cycle constructor, target-avoidance theorem, CT1 trace, totality, and zero-check audit. The Erdős layer only retains the selected power-of-two avoiding context.

proofSteps.title: Certificate-marked fan degree cap
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This implements the degree-at-most-eight clause of the manuscript lemma. The at-most-seven strengthening is implemented by the following CT9 stage; the certificate-closed charge calculation remains downstream.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The marked-fan contract supplies a legal nonempty P13 label for each actual incident port and pairwise C2 compatibility. The graph layer chooses the least position in each label. A cached eight-slot cover puts only equal or distance-four positions in one slot, so compatibility makes the slot map injective. CT9 scans those eight fibres and proves the fan has at most eight neighbours.

proofSteps.title: Non-singleton marked-fan cap
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This completes the manuscript's at-most-seven packing clause. The subsequent certificate-closed charge calculation is implemented by the following CT14 stage.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Choose two distinct positions in the distinguished marked label. Their closed conflict neighbourhood blocks two different representative slots. The graph layer erases that one fan port, gives the blocked slots capacity zero and each other slot capacity one, and runs CT9. At most six other labels remain, so the full fan has degree at most seven.

manuscriptRefs.title: Fan label packing and certificate-closed charge
manuscriptRefs.label: lem:fan-certificate

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned two-blocked-slot refinement
declarationGroups.explanation: The generic CT9 layer owns absent zero-capacity fibres and the bounded run. The graph layer owns erased-item bookkeeping, the cached blocked-slot certificate, exact capacity six, and the seven-member conclusion. The Erdős layer identifies the actual port and two positions.

proofSteps.workBound: CT14 makes a constant number of passes over exactly k actual incident ports. No subset of ports, cubic-closed assignment, attachment-label family, path, subgraph, context, or graph universe is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Certificate-closed fan charge
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This completes the certificate-closed charge clause of the fan-certificate lemma. The positive-deficit hybrid entry is already locally verified, but its marked-fan degree input and the global B1/B2 bridge are not yet composed in one downstream stage.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: CT14 scans every actual fan port and sums the cubic-closed indicator to obtain c. The remaining k-c ports are open. In quarter units the center contributes 11-4k, each closed port contributes -1, and each open port contributes at least 3, so the exact lower bound is 11-k-4c. The defining certificate-closed inequality 4c+k≤11 makes this nonnegative.

manuscriptRefs.title: Fan label packing and certificate-closed charge
manuscriptRefs.label: lem:fan-certificate

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned exact fan charge ledger
declarationGroups.explanation: The graph layer computes cubic closure from the two literal non-center assigned incidences, computes the exact assigned-incidence quarter weight, and owns the finite closed/open CT14 scan and integer lower-bound arithmetic. The Erdős layer supplies its assignment and later proves the external-correction transfer to induced-core charge.

proofSteps.workBound: The mass and incidence CT14 stages use only the actual cubic-closed subtype and exactly two incidences per member, with the existing bound 4n²+20n+1. No port subsets, label families, candidate ledgers, paths, subgraphs, contexts, or graphs are enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Positive-deficit fan-closed entry
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This composes the previously separate marked-fan degree cap and local hybrid incidence execution for the actual two-fan-closed-port branch. Global disjointness between entries belonging to different fan centres is the subsequent B2 problem and is not claimed here.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Two compatible fan-closed ports give two distinct actual cubic-closed neighbours. The marked-fan CT9 theorem supplies k≤8 rather than accepting it as a local premise. Hence the exact quarter-deficit 4c+k-11 is positive, while the two real non-centre incidences of every cubic-closed neighbour form an endpoint-disjoint CT14 ledger whose half-credits pay that deficit with three quarter-units of slack.

declarationGroups.title: Composed marked-fan and incidence CT14 stage
declarationGroups.explanation: The Erdős data are only the actual marked fan, assigned-incidence predicate, and two compatible fan-closed ports. The existing framework mass and hybrid-incidence profiles derive the closed count, positive deficit, exact CT14 execution, endpoint-disjoint credit, and work bound; the marked-fan degree theorem is consumed directly.

proofSteps.title: Local hybrid fan entry realizes B1
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This proves the local B1 identification only. Pairwise disjointness of carriers belonging to different fan centers is the subsequent finite B2 problem.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The positive-deficit CT14 result is converted to a framework semantic ledger containing exactly the five facts used by B1: terminal capacity, 2c literal incidences, injective endpoint carriers, the exact window/non-window partition, and payment of the total and residual demands.

proofSteps.workBound: The concrete all-incidence witness makes one linear pass over exactly 2c literal incidences. Finiteness is proved by an injective subtype argument over Finset; no powerset, candidate product, demand subset, path, subgraph, context, or graph universe is evaluated.
proofSteps.title: Concrete positive-deficit candidate fibre
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is the exact positive-deficit candidate fibre and a constructive sufficient existence case. It does not assume a candidate-validity contract. The certificate-closed candidate fibre and the cross-centre choice/overlap argument are the next stages.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A candidate is now a proved finite subset of the literal fan-incidence universe, rather than an application-supplied validity predicate. It must contain every packed-window incidence, avoid every incidence occupied by the ordinary reserve, and select enough non-window half-credits to pay the exact remaining demand. If the reserve occupies none of these incidences, the verified local B1 inequality constructs the all-incidence candidate.
proofSteps.formalStatement: I_W\subseteq S,\quad S\cap R_{\mathrm{ord}}=\varnothing,\quad 2|S\cap I_N|\ge D_N

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned weighted finite selection
declarationGroups.explanation: The core defines finite mandatory/forbidden weighted selections without evaluating a powerset. The graph layer specializes the item universe and support to literal hybrid fan incidences. The Erdős layer is a thin instantiation.

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {base : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun x => Unit}
  {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V} {baseline : base.problem.Baseline object} {center : V}
  (fanProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.FanWindowProfile V)
  (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.IncidenceReserve V),
  (∑ incidence ∈ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.profile fanProfile reserve).allItems,
      if
          StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.incidenceKind fanProfile incidence =
            StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.IncidenceKind.nonWindow then
        2
      else 0) =
    ↑(StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.nonWindowQuarterCredit fanProfile)
declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.allItems_weight_eq_nonWindowQuarterCredit

            (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.IncidenceReserve V) →
              object.degree center ≤ 8 →
                (∀ (incidence : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Incidence fanProfile),
                    ¬reserve.Used (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.carrier fanProfile incidence)) →
                  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanCandidate.Candidate fanProfile reserve

proofSteps.workBound: The constructive witness scans exactly k actual fan ports. The candidate fibre is logically finite by the common subtype theorem; no powerset, candidate product, demand subset, path, subgraph, context, or graph universe is evaluated.
proofSteps.title: Concrete certificate-closed candidate fibre
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Both local Type B candidate predicates are now concrete framework definitions. The next stage forms the actual dependent demand family from these two fibres and only then applies CT12.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A candidate is a proved subset of the actual neighbour-port list. Its assigned-incidence weight is q=11-4(1+a). Decorative or packed-window incidences outside the counted core contribute an exact external correction, so Lean proves q+4e equals the actual induced-core quarter charge and hence q is a safe lower bound. The selected total offsets the exact center charge 11-4k. Reserve-used vertices are forbidden, and every selected endpoint is a literal adjacent degree-three non-center vertex.

manuscriptRefs.title: Finite completion of the Type B support assignment
manuscriptRefs.label: lem:typeB-maximal-completion

declarationGroups.title: Framework-owned exact fan-neighbour selection
declarationGroups.explanation: The graph layer computes assigned-degree member weights and owns the center demand, finite candidate subtype, reserve exclusion, carrier support, and complete-neighbour witness. The induced-core transfer, including exact external-shoulder correction, is proved separately from literal graph assignment. The Erdős layer identifies members with actual ports and derives the N(h) minus H_X condition from adjacency and deletion criticality.

declarations.type:
∀ {Member : Type uMember} {Vertex : Type uVertex}
  (chargeProfile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCharge.Profile Member)
  (vertexDecidableEq : DecidableEq Vertex) (center : Vertex) (vertex : Member → Vertex)
  (reserve : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCandidate.VertexReserve Vertex)
  {problem : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext problem),
  -↑(chargeProfile.closedCount context) + 3 * ↑(chargeProfile.openCount context) ≤
    ∑
      member ∈
        (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.CertificateClosedFanCandidate.Profile.selectionProfile chargeProfile
            vertexDecidableEq center vertex reserve).allItems,
      chargeProfile.quarterCharge member

proofSteps.workBound: The schedule is a filtered subtype of the explicit vertex enumeration. Candidate finiteness is proof-level; declared support scans each local item list once. No candidate product or demand powerset is evaluated.
proofSteps.title: Concrete dependent Type B demand family
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is complete for every declared assigned-center family. Completeness of that family relative to a raw vertex support is proved separately by the high-center resolution split.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A declared assigned support maps each center to either its certificate-closed local entry or its positive-B1 local entry. That branch value definitionally determines the literal item universe and weighted selection profile. The common adapter derives candidates, finiteness, selected support, and the complete declared support used by overlap.

declarationGroups.title: Framework-derived heterogeneous ledger
declarationGroups.explanation: The core adapter owns the dependent candidate and support fields. The Erdős specialization only selects one already verified literal fan profile at each declared center.

proofSteps.workBound: Exactly one list-peeling iteration per declared center and therefore at most |V(G)| iterations. Choice and minimality are proof-level; candidate products and demand subsets are never materialized.
proofSteps.title: CT12 full choice or minimal overlap
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This proves completeness of the fixed declared demand schedule. It intentionally does not identify that statement with residual-core maximality or decorated-handoff closure.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: CT12 peels the entire declared center schedule. If all demands admit mutually disjoint candidates, Lean returns a full dependent choice. Otherwise it proves a nonempty full obstruction and selects a least-cardinality obstructing subschedule, every proper shorter nonempty subschedule of which has a disjoint choice.

proofSteps.workBound: Support construction is linear in the declared local item universes. Minimal obstruction selection is proof-level Nat.find and performs no subfamily enumeration.
proofSteps.title: Minimal overlap support and inherited graph constraints
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The exact support, contextual target avoidance, high-center separation, cubic-neighbour clause, both direct window exclusions, literal nonempty-boundary CT3 compression exclusion, minimal-overlap clause, and impossibility of a carrier-separated demand split are proved. Turning the last combinatorial theorem into induced-graph connectivity, plus the window-stub global count and delocalization routing, requires additional graph structures.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: On failure of the full choice, the exact CT12 obstruction is packaged with the union of the declared carrier universes of its demands. This union retains a reserve-blocked demand even if it has no valid candidate. Every demand center lies in the union; ambient target avoidance, independence of high centers, cubicity of their neighbours, and proper-subfamily ledger choices are proved directly.

proofSteps.workBound: The high-center schedule is a linear filter of the explicit vertex enumeration. The dependent resolution and choice split are proof-level and do not enumerate witness products.
proofSteps.title: Total high-center state-space stratification
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This closes the completeness gap for ordinary high-center demands in a supplied vertex scope. A local-entry failure is an explicit theorem branch, not an assumed contract. The distinct decorated-handoff and later fan-mass closures remain separate manuscript branches.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Starting only from a literal vertex support and reserve, Lean filters all high centers. Either one actual center has no certificate-closed or positive-B1 local entry, or proof-level finite choice builds the complete assigned family and CT12 returns a full disjoint ledger or a minimal overlap obstruction. Thus local-entry existence is no longer silently accepted as a support field.

          Internal.VerifiedTypeBResolutionPrefix ctx

proofSteps.workBound: All constructions are proof-level finset images, filters, unions, sums, and monotone Fin embeddings over declared centers, ports, selected incidences, and the actual remaining vertex set. No powerset, injection space, candidate product, graph family, path family, or context universe is evaluated.
proofSteps.title: Exact Type B graph-charge realization
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The official quantitative endpoint is choice-free: unresolved local entries and B2 overlap cannot evade the charge bound. The only remaining term is the exact total overload of the proof-selected receiver fibres in the literal center-deleted Type A graph. The finer full-choice theorem and its 800-unit selected-envelope bound remain valid refinements, not assumptions in the unconditional result.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: For a full disjoint local choice, Lean names the exact core vertices consumed at every center. Certificate weights transfer to selected core neighbours; window credit transfers through external assigned shoulders; selected non-window credit transfers only through canonical ordinary available vertices. These supports lie inside the candidate carriers, so CT12 disjointness prevents reuse. The graph layer then partitions the counted core into used vertices, retained high centers, and the remaining core and proves the exact reduced-ledger identity. Separately, deleting all actual high centers gives the choice-free receiver-overload bound on every literal Type B scope.

manuscriptRefs.title: Quantitative Type B reduction under the refined ledger
manuscriptRefs.label: prop:typeB-bridge-reduction

declarationGroups.title: Framework graph ledger and Erdős literal transfer
declarationGroups.explanation: The reusable graph profile owns the exact post-selection partition and reduced-ledger algebra. The application proves that its local candidate resources are literal, core-contained, center-free subsets of the framework carriers.

proofSteps.workBound: CT12 performs exactly n-1 peeling iterations on one proof-selected vertex list and has a linear polynomial check budget. The edge theorem uses well-founded recursion on strictly smaller explicit supports; no graph, subgraph, order, path, or context universe is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Sparse upper envelope and slack identity
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This verifies the complete node [126] block, including both displayed identities. Nodes [127]–[128] remain the already verified CT6 surplus/activation outputs; the first later sparse-branch datum not yet implemented is node [129]'s baseline spine demand.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A cubic vertex is obtained from the already verified deletion-critical graph. Every induced core in its complement is a proper subgraph of the selected counterexample, so the no-proper-core theorem produces a two-degenerate elimination certificate. CT12 checks precisely that finite order. Counting at most two deleted edges until the last two vertices gives e(G-v)≤2(n-1)-3; restoring the three incident edges gives m≤2n-2. The graph handshake theorem identifies the CT6 excess ledger with σ=2m-3n, and elementary integer algebra gives σ=n-6-2λ.

declarationGroups.title: Two-degenerate graph profile, exact CT12 run, and Erdős arithmetic
declarationGroups.explanation: The reusable graph layer proves existence of the bounded elimination order from local induced-core freeness, executes CT12 on that list, proves the sharp edge count, and connects the ordered degree-excess total to the handshake identity. The Erdős layer supplies only the selected cubic vertex and the concrete no-proper-core specialization.

proofSteps.workBound: CT15 makes two passes over the declared coordinate list plus one comparison. For the canonical empty family this is one constant check; no subsets, target states, graph families, or contexts are enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Baseline spine demand
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This verifies the exact node [129] definition and makes its deficit explicit. The manuscript does not derive an O(n) deficit from that definition; a nonempty coordinate construction with a separately verified linear deficit remains necessary before the entropy sandwich can imply a near-cubic bound.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [129] defines a baseline demand as an independently target-testable finite coordinate family together with its deficit from the cubic skeleton budget. The canonical empty family is an unconditional instance: its exact deficit is the entire executable cubic bit budget. CT15 scans that complete declared family, proves full rank, and returns its exact ledger. This implements the definition without assuming the separate linear-deficit estimate needed by the later entropy sandwich.

declarationGroups.title: Exact finite baseline-demand profile and CT15 ledger
declarationGroups.explanation: The framework layer owns certified independent finite demands, their unit-charge full-rank ledgers, deterministic CT15 terminal and trace, soundness, totality, and linear work. The Erdős layer supplies only the exact cubic skeleton count and the canonical empty family with its explicit full deficit.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
∀ {V : Type u_1} {base : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun x => Unit}
  {object : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V} {baseline : base.problem.Baseline object} {center : V}
  (centerHigh : 4 ≤ object.degree center)
  (deletionCritical : ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), object.degree dart.toProd.1 = 3 ∨ object.degree dart.toProd.2 = 3)
  (profile : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.FanWindowProfile V)
  (first second : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.OpenPort centerHigh deletionCritical)
  (pair : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FanClosedPort.CompatiblePair centerHigh deletionCritical first second)

  (fourFree :
    ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HasCycleWithLength object.graph
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HighCenterStructure.FourLength)
  (degreeLeEight : object.degree center ≤ 8),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HybridFanIncidence.VerifiedStage centerHigh deletionCritical profile first second pair
    assigned fourFree degreeLeEight
